revision.text:
[[ja:有川神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Arikawa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kami-goto.arikawa.JPG |location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}} Minamimatsuura District, Shin-Kamigotō Town, Arikawa 1073-1 |Position = {{coord|32|58|50.8|N|129|07|19.2|E|region:JP-42_scale:25000_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} |Deities = {{ill|Izanami Ōkami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Gōsha (village shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |Establishment = Shōwa 62 (1987) |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |festival = October 2 |Sacred rites = Arikawa Kagura (Gion Festival, festival) |location = {{ill|Shinkamigoto|qid=Q1206790|1=Shin-Kamigotō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.980778|N|129.122|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Arikawa Shrine''' (有川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shin-Kamigotō|en|Shin-Kamigotō|qid=Q1206790|lt=Shin-Kamigotō|1=Shin-Kamigotō}}, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|en|Nagasaki Prefecture|qid=Q169376|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. It was established through the merger of three shrines in Arikawa: '''Ubagimi Shrine''' (祖母君神社), '''Tenman Shrine''' (天満神社), and '''Hachiman Shrine''' (八幡神社). == Enshrined Deities == The main enshrined deities are {{ill|Izanami no Mikoto|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, along with nine auxiliary deities: {{ill|Susanoo no Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q272993}}, {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8009611}} (two deities), {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, {{ill|Watatsumi no Kami|ja|海童神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505729|1=Watatsumi}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|天穂日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノホヒ}}, {{ill|Tachikarao no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachikarao no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Mizuhanome no Kami|ja|水波能売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8191796}}. The enshrined deities were inherited from the original shrines: Izanami no Mikoto and Susanoo no Mikoto from Ubagimi Shrine, Sugawara no Michizane from Tenman Shrine, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} from Hachiman Shrine. == History == The three shrines in Arikawa—Ubagimi Shrine (former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}), Tenman Shrine (former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}), and Hachiman Shrine (former {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}})—were merged due to concerns over population decline and aging in the area. On September 5, Shōwa 60 (1985), the decision was made by a joint meeting of shrine representatives to establish Arikawa Shrine. The new shrine was constructed on the site of Tenman Shrine, and the three shrines' deities were collectively enshrined. The new shrine was inaugurated on April 24, Shōwa 62 (1987). == Rituals and Festivals == The Gion Festival and annual festival on October 1–2 feature the performance of {{ill|Arikawa Kagura|ja|有川神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arikawa Kagura|1=Arikawa Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11516390}}, which is part of the {{ill|Goto Kagura|ja|五島神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goto Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a National Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property. ;Major Rituals and Festivals * {{ill|New Year’s Festival|ja|元旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year’s Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳旦祭|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546824}} (January 1) * {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} (February 3) * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (February 17) * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} (3rd Friday and Saturday in July) * {{ill|Reisai|ja|例祭|fr|Reisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル}} (October 1–2) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 23) * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae-shiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (December 31) == Overview of Merged Shrines == === Ubagimi Shrine === Located in the Nakasuji area of Arikawa, Ubagimi Shrine was associated with the nearby {{ill|Sofukimi Shrine|ja|祖父君神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sofukimi Shrine|1=Ojigimi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588528}} in {{ill|Urasuwa|ja|浦桑郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urasuwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine was referred to as the "Lower Shrine" while Sofukimi Shrine was the "Upper Shrine." === Tenman Shrine === Located in the Kamiarikawa area, Tenman Shrine enshrined {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} as its main deity. === Hachiman Shrine === Located in the Hama area, Hachiman Shrine was dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|誉田別神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. It served as the guardian shrine of the Arikawa whaling community, symbolized by the "Hachi" (eight) character in its flags. == Other Shrines in Arikawa == Unmerged shrines in the area include {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Shin-Kamigotō)|ja|金比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine (Shin-Kamigotō)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅神社|1=Kotohira shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646488}}, {{ill|Ōkunushi Shrine|ja|大地主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkunushi Shrine|1=Otokone Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11433686}}, and {{ill|Masatoshi Shrine|ja|正利翁社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masatoshi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Nearby areas feature {{ill|Shiome Tenman Shrine|ja|潮目天満神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiome Tenman Shrine|1=Shiome Tenman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11565730}}, {{ill|Omiya Shrine (Shin-Kamigotō)|ja|乙宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omiya Shrine (Shin-Kamigotō)|1=Otomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369923}}, and {{ill|Shijikihaguro Shrine|ja|志自岐羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijikihaguro Shrine|1=Shijikihaguro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11491128}}. File:IMG_kamiari_konpira.JPG|Konpira Shrine File:IMG_otokonushi.JPG|Ōkunushi Shrine File:IMG_ogawara.JPG|Omiya Shrine (Ogawara) == References == * 『有川町郷土誌 平成6th year 』 Arikawa Town Local History Editorial Committee * 『有川村郷土誌 大正8th year （復刻版）』 * 『海童神社と有川祭り』 Fumiro Araki, 2009 * 『五島神社誌 昭和18th year （復刻版）』 Shinto Association, Minamimatsuura District {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|有川神社|version=97032625|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:有川神社]] {{Commons category|Arikawa-jinja (Shinkamigoto)}} {{wikidata link|Q11516392}} [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:新上五島町の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
